REST Visit-Time Clean Analysis

Scheduled and recorded unscheduled visit utilization, rebuilt from the raw visit-date/minute workbook with clear denominators and calendar-time definitions.

Generated 2026-05-12Primary N=108No missing time set to zero
Participant rows122after excluding summary rows
Primary cohort108valid VV1A anchor
Sensitivity cohort85valid VV1A + 6m date
Scheduled minutes50,948months 0-6
Unscheduled minutes9,672months 0-6
Unscheduled events293dated events, months 0-6

Overview

Clean scheduled and recorded unscheduled visit utilization analysis from the raw visit workbook. The primary denominator is participants with valid VV1A because calendar time requires a start anchor.

How to interpret

  • Primary cohort: N=108 with valid VV1A.
  • Sensitivity cohort: N=85 with valid VV1A and valid 6-month in-person date.
  • Monthly displays are minutes-first; visit counts are supporting context.

Source population

122 participant rows were identified after excluding 4 workbook summary rows.

Scheduled visits

Scheduled protocol contacts are summarized by visit sequence and by actual calendar month since VV1A.

Unscheduled events

Unscheduled analyses use actual event dates for calendar timing. Slot number is event order only.

Manual adjudication

Recorded unscheduled events may include true extra visits and make-up/rescheduled visits; the event review tab supports adjudication.

Recorded-minute caveat: minute totals reflect recorded numeric clinical minutes only. Missing or non-numeric minute fields were not imputed or set to zero, and recorded unscheduled events still require manual adjudication before being interpreted as true extra clinical need.

Methods and Denominators

Definitions used to prevent confusion between visit order, calendar timing, and clinical time burden.

Guardrails

  • Scheduled protocol table uses protocol visit order.
  • Monthly tables use actual dates relative to VV1A.
  • Unscheduled slots are not months.
Primary cohort: N=108 with valid VV1A, used for main utilization displays.
Sensitivity: N=85 with valid VV1A and valid 6-month in-person date.
Month buckets: 0-30, 31-60, 61-90, 91-120, 121-150, and 151-183 days from VV1A.
Important caveat: do not call all recorded unscheduled events true extra clinical-need visits until manual review distinguishes make-up/rescheduled visits.
Minute totals: recorded numeric minutes are summed; missing/non-numeric minute values are flagged and not imputed.

Scheduled Visits by Protocol Sequence

Clinical minutes at planned scheduled visits through VV6A.

Key interpretation

  • Early scheduled contacts carry the largest clinical-time burden.
  • The figure is by protocol sequence, not calendar month.
  • N labels are numeric-minute denominators at each visit.

Scheduled protocol visit clinical time

Solid line is median; ribbon is IQR; dashed orange line is mean.

Scientific interpretation

  • Use this for the planned protocol visit sequence.
  • This is the most appropriate display for whether early scheduled visits took longer than later visits.

Scheduled Visit Time by Calendar Month

Total scheduled clinical minutes by actual scheduled visit date relative to VV1A.

Key interpretation

  • This is the calendar-time scheduled workload display.
  • Month 0-1 is highest because several planned protocol contacts occur early.
  • Y-axis is total minutes, not visit counts.

Scheduled visit minutes by month

Primary cohort N=108; y-axis is total scheduled clinical minutes.

Scientific interpretation

  • This directly answers scheduled time used by month.
  • Visit-occurrence counts are retained in the table but are not the primary display.

Recorded Unscheduled Events by Calendar Month

Unscheduled visit burden using actual unscheduled event dates relative to VV1A.

Key interpretation

  • The primary display is total unscheduled minutes by month.
  • Counts distinguish event occurrences from unique participants.
  • These are recorded unscheduled events, not yet adjudicated true extra visits.

Recorded unscheduled minutes by month

Y-axis is total unscheduled clinical minutes by actual event date.

Scientific interpretation

  • Use this figure for unscheduled time burden.
  • This avoids confusing slot number with month.

Recorded unscheduled event counts by month

Event occurrences and unique participants are shown as supporting context.

Scientific interpretation

  • Counts can exceed the number of people because participants can have multiple events.
  • This plot helps explain concentration of events across participants.

Interspersed Scheduled + Unscheduled Timeline

Scheduled and recorded unscheduled contacts are combined into one date-ordered first-6-month workload stream while preserving event type.

Key interpretation

  • This is the most direct display of total clinical workload over time.
  • Teal is planned scheduled time; orange is recorded unscheduled time layered on top.
  • Missing minutes are not imputed, and unscheduled events remain recorded events pending adjudication.

Interspersed monthly clinical time

Stacked monthly total of scheduled and recorded unscheduled clinical minutes.

Scientific interpretation

  • Use this as the cleanest answer to “how much total clinical time was used each month?”
  • The orange segment shows how much additional recorded unscheduled time sits on top of planned care.

Participant-level interspersed workload

Each point is one participant: scheduled minutes on the x-axis and recorded unscheduled minutes on the y-axis.

Scientific interpretation

  • This shows whether unscheduled time is broadly distributed or concentrated among participants with higher contact burden.
  • Participants with zero unscheduled minutes remain in the plot rather than being dropped.

Timing Relationship: Scheduled vs Unscheduled

These figures use exact day-from-VV1A timestamps to show how recorded unscheduled contacts sit around planned scheduled care.

Key interpretation

  • Use this tab to assess timing, not only monthly totals.
  • Near-zero distance to scheduled visits suggests clustering around planned care.
  • Events far from scheduled visits may reflect independent extra contact burden, pending adjudication.

Patient-level event timing raster

Each row is a participant sorted by recorded unscheduled burden; points use exact days from VV1A.

Scientific interpretation

  • This is the best visual for seeing whether unscheduled events cluster within individuals over time.
  • The plot preserves all primary-cohort participants and does not collapse visits into months.

Weekly combined workload

Exact dates are grouped into study weeks to show finer-grained workload than monthly buckets.

Scientific interpretation

  • This helps identify specific high-workload periods that are hidden by monthly aggregation.
  • Stacked bars retain scheduled versus recorded unscheduled time.

Distance from unscheduled event to nearest scheduled visit

Each recorded unscheduled event is compared with the nearest scheduled visit for the same participant.

Scientific interpretation

  • Negative values mean the unscheduled event occurred before the nearest scheduled visit; positive values mean after.
  • This is the cleanest descriptive test of whether unscheduled events cluster around scheduled contacts.

Cumulative clinical time over 183 days

Curves show how recorded scheduled, unscheduled, and total clinical minutes accumulate from VV1A.

Scientific interpretation

  • This shows whether unscheduled burden accumulates steadily or in bursts.
  • The final cumulative total reconciles to the combined monthly total.

Timing windows for recorded unscheduled events

Participant-Level Unscheduled Burden

How recorded unscheduled events are distributed across participants in the primary cohort.

Key interpretation

  • This distinguishes widespread light use from concentrated high use.
  • Zeros are retained for participants with no dated unscheduled events within days 0-183.
  • Burden categories support clinical review and resource planning.

Participant-level recorded unscheduled burden

Counts of participants with 0, 1, 2, or at least 3 events.

Scientific interpretation

  • Use this to show how many participants had repeated recorded unscheduled contacts.
  • This remains descriptive until true extra versus make-up visits are adjudicated.

Supplemental Unscheduled Slot Analysis

Clinical time by sequential unscheduled event slot. This is event order, not calendar timing.

Key interpretation

  • Use only as supplemental context.
  • Do not interpret slot 6 as month 6.
  • Sparse later slots are flagged.

Supplemental unscheduled slot time

Median/IQR and mean by event-order slot.

Scientific interpretation

  • This shows time per sequential recorded event among participants with repeated events.
  • Calendar timing should come from the monthly tab, not this slot plot.

6-Month Completer Sensitivity

Key tables repeated among participants with valid VV1A and valid 6-month in-person date.

Why include this

  • The primary analysis reflects those who started the virtual visit sequence.
  • The completer sensitivity helps compare with prior 6-month analyses.
  • Interpret primary and sensitivity denominators separately.

QC and Manual Review

Audit material for reconciling definitions and preparing manual adjudication of unscheduled events.

Use this tab for questions

  • Event-level review table is in the Excel workbook.
  • Manual classification is intentionally blank for Molly/team adjudication.
  • Validation checks passed at build time.
Validation: primary monthly scheduled and unscheduled totals reconcile to raw long-format rows. Workbook summary rows were excluded.
CheckExpected/LogicResultStatus
Primary cohort Nvalid VV1A108.0PASS
Sensitivity cohort Nvalid VV1A + valid 6m85.0PASS
